title: anthemis scrobicularis subsp. fungosa
intro: Anthemis scrobicularis subsp. fungosa is a subspecies of the Anthemis scrobicularis plant, also known as a type of yarrow or mountain yarrow, which is a flowering plant in the sunflower family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Anthemis scrobicularis subsp. fungosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as a natural antiseptic for wounds and infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are typically used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
